programs.name: Medical Assistance
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including long-term care services. In Pennsylvania, it is administered through the state Department of Human Services.
programs.who_its_for: Pennsylvania residents aged 65+ or disabled with low income and assets meeting state Medicaid thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, and nursing home care with no or low copays.

programs.name: Community HealthChoices
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services waivers to help seniors and disabled individuals avoid institutional care. Provides personalized care plans including personal assistance and adult day services.
programs.who_its_for: Pennsylvania residents aged 65+ or 21+ with disabilities needing nursing facility level of care but preferring home-based services.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $50,000+ annually in services depending on assessed needs; no cost for most eligible.

programs.name: PACE/PACENET
programs.what_it_does: Pennsylvania's Program for All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) provides comprehensive medical and social services. PACENET extends prescription coverage for those with higher incomes via premiums and copays.
programs.who_its_for: PA residents 65+ needing nursing home level care but able to live safely in community; income up to $23,500 single/$31,500 couple for PACENET.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage for PACE; $8 generics/$15 brand copays after premium for PACENET.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and copays for eligible low-income seniors. Known as MSP; includes QMB (full coverage), SLMB, and QI variants.
programs.who_its_for: PA Medicare beneficiaries 65+ with incomes typically under $1,300-$1,500/month single (varies by type) and limited assets.
programs.benefit_value: Saves $2,000-$5,000/year in premiums/costs; QMB covers all Medicare cost-sharing.

programs.name: SNAP
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card for food purchases to low-income households. In PA, administered as Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
programs.who_its_for: PA seniors 60+ with household income below 130% federal poverty level (~$1,951/month single).

programs.name: LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program grants cash payments to offset heating/cooling costs. Administered yearly in PA with crisis grants available.
programs.who_its_for: PA households with income ≤150% poverty (~$22,590 single) including seniors/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000 one-time grant depending on fuel type, income, household size.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Free home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs. Part of PA's home heating/weatherization initiatives for low-income.
programs.who_its_for: PA low-income households (≤200% poverty) with seniors, disabled, or children prioritized.
programs.benefit_value: $3,000-$7,000 in free improvements per home, reducing energy bills 20-30%.

programs.name: SHIP
programs.what_it_does: State Health Insurance Assistance Program offers free counseling on Medicare, Medigap, and savings programs. PA's version helps navigate options and appeals.
programs.who_its_for: All PA Medicare beneficiaries 65+, spouses, and caregivers seeking insurance guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps access $1,000s in annual savings.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors, often Meals on Wheels branded locally. Funded via Older Americans Act through PA Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: PA residents 60+ unable to shop/prepare meals due to illness or disability.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at suggested $3 donation; free for lowest-income.

programs.name: Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, counseling, and training for family caregivers of seniors. Includes financial assistance for relief services.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid caregivers of PA residents 60+ or 18+ with dementia/Alzheimer's.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $600/month respite reimbursement; counseling and support groups.

programs.name: SCSEP
programs.what_it_does: Senior Community Service Employment Program offers part-time paid training jobs. Federal program run via PA Department of Aging partners.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed PA residents 55+ with income ≤125% poverty seeking work.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work (avg $10/hr) up to 20 hrs/week for training leading to employment.

programs.name: Legal Counsel for the Elderly
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice and representation for income-eligible seniors. PA-specific name from state senior services brochure.
programs.who_its_for: PA residents 60+ with low income facing issues like housing, benefits, abuse.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living, resolving complaints. Operates statewide via PA Department of Aging.
programs.who_its_for: PA long-term care residents, families, and staff seeking issue resolution.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; resolves 80%+ complaints without litigation.
